profile_table.Predictability: Fast but more variable than predictability-first alternatives
profile_table.Overhead source & lever: Distributed overhead profile; optimize entry/setup and execution path before residual tail
profile_table.Verdict & deployment: Generally usable with healthier success profile; mainly push-associated

profile_table.Speed profile: Mixed-speed case: early entry can be competitive, but completion tail is heavy
profile_table.Overhead source & lever: Tail-heavy mixed case; reduce bespoke post-execution work and standardize orchestration
profile_table.Verdict & deployment: Sparse and context-sensitive; low observed success and often pull_request-associated

profile_table.Overhead source & lever: Execution-centric profile; first optimization target is the main execution path
profile_table.Verdict & deployment: Most usable and success-oriented outcomes; mainly schedule-associated

profile_table.Speed profile: Slow sustained-execution profile with heavy entry burden
profile_table.Overhead source & lever: Heavy entry plus heavy execution; optimize provisioning delay and provider-side execution cost
profile_table.Verdict & deployment: Usable verdicts common, but success strongly trigger-conditioned; mainly schedule-associated

observations.obs_title: Community is the fastest overall operational profile
observations.question: Which style is the fastest overall operational profile?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on study_run_duration_seconds: p=6.32e-66, epsilon^2=0.035; vs Custom: p_adj=0.000399, rbc=0.311, vs GMD: p_adj=5.04e-11, rbc=0.231, vs Third-Party: p_adj=2.91e-56, rbc=0.412. stored='Community', winner='Community'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.

observations.obs_title: GMD reaches the instrumentation path fastest, but does not finish fastest
observations.question: Which style shows the clearest fast-entry profile without being the fastest overall finisher?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on study_run_duration_seconds: p=6.32e-66, epsilon^2=0.035; vs Community: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.231, vs Custom: p_adj=2.48e-08, rbc=0.551, vs Third-Party: p_adj=3.13e-64, rbc=0.726. stored='GMD', winner='Community'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.

observations.obs_title: Third-Party is the slowest sustained-execution profile
observations.question: Which style is the slowest sustained-execution profile?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on study_invocation_execution_window_selected_stage3_seconds: p=7.71e-51, epsilon^2=0.040; vs Community: p_adj=8.4e-33, rbc=0.630, vs Custom: p_adj=2.93e-08, rbc=0.609, vs GMD: p_adj=2.22e-11, rbc=0.474. stored='Third-Party', winner='Third-Party'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.

observations.obs_title: Custom shows a mixed speed profile
observations.question: Which style shows a mixed speed profile with competitive entry, middling core path, and a long completion tail?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on study_post_invocation_selected_stage3_seconds: p=1.13e-71, epsilon^2=0.056; vs Community: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.721, vs Custom: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.802, vs Third-Party: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.040. stored='GMD', winner='GMD'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored as mixed speed profile: competitive entry/execution with heavier post-invocation tail.

observations.obs_title: Community’s speed advantage is concentrated in the core execution windows and comes with a longer tail
observations.question: Which style combines a fast core execution profile with a longer residual tail?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on study_invocation_execution_window_selected_stage3_seconds: p=7.71e-51, epsilon^2=0.040; vs Community: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.469, vs Custom: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.196, vs Third-Party: p_adj=3.33e-11, rbc=0.474. stored='GMD', winner='GMD'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored by fast core execution plus longer post-invocation tail.

observations.obs_title: GMD is the most predictable style on the main completion-oriented measures
observations.question: Which style is the most predictable on the main completion-oriented measures?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on study_run_duration_seconds: p=6.32e-66, epsilon^2=0.035; vs Community: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.231, vs Custom: p_adj=2.48e-08, rbc=0.551, vs Third-Party: p_adj=3.13e-64, rbc=0.726. stored='GMD', winner='GMD'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored by lowest average normalized MAD across completion-oriented timing measures.

observations.obs_title: Community is fast in typical terms, but predictability-poor
observations.question: Which style is fast in typical terms but predictability-poor?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on study_run_duration_seconds: p=6.32e-66, epsilon^2=0.035; vs Community: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.412, vs Custom: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.139, vs GMD: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.726. stored='Third-Party', winner='Third-Party'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored by fast typical runtime combined with poor predictability.

observations.obs_title: Third-Party carries the strongest absolute tail-risk profile
observations.question: Which style carries the strongest absolute tail-risk profile?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on study_run_duration_seconds: p=6.32e-66, epsilon^2=0.035; vs Community: p_adj=1.94e-56, rbc=0.412, vs Custom: p_adj=0.074, rbc=0.139, vs GMD: p_adj=3.13e-64, rbc=0.726. stored='Third-Party', winner='Third-Party'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored by highest average p90 across main completion/tail timing measures.

observations.obs_title: Custom has a mixed predictability profile and should be interpreted cautiously
observations.question: Which style shows a mixed predictability profile that should be interpreted cautiously?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on study_run_duration_seconds: p=6.32e-66, epsilon^2=0.035; vs Community: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.311, vs GMD: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.551, vs Third-Party: p_adj=0.222, rbc=0.139. stored='Custom', winner='Custom'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored by predictability closeness to a middling profile as a proxy for mixed/cautious predictability.

observations.obs_title: GMD is the clearest execution-centric style
observations.question: Which style is the clearest execution-centric overhead profile?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on execution_window_share: p=1.23e-52, epsilon^2=0.041; vs Community: p_adj=4.03e-47, rbc=0.702, vs Custom: p_adj=7.66e-16, rbc=0.880, vs Third-Party: p_adj=8.66e-28, rbc=0.775. stored='GMD', winner='GMD'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored by high execution-window share and low post-invocation share.

observations.obs_title: Third-Party is best characterized by heavy entry and heavy execution, not by a dominant completion tail
observations.question: Which style is best characterized by heavy entry plus heavy execution rather than a dominant completion tail?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on execution_window_share: p=1.23e-52, epsilon^2=0.041; vs GMD: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.775, vs Community: p_adj=3.39e-09, rbc=0.315, vs Custom: p_adj=0.000535, rbc=0.389. stored='Third-Party', winner='GMD'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored by high pre-invocation + execution share with lower post share.

observations.obs_title: Community has a distributed overhead profile rather than a single dominant overhead source
observations.question: Which style has a distributed overhead profile rather than a single dominant overhead source?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on execution_window_share: p=1.23e-52, epsilon^2=0.041; vs Custom: p_adj=1, rbc=0.012, vs GMD: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.702, vs Third-Party: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.315. stored='Community', winner='Custom'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored by the lowest maximum phase-share as a distributed-overhead proxy.

observations.obs_title: Custom remains a cautious, tail-heavy mixed case
observations.question: Which style remains a cautious tail-heavy mixed overhead case?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Kruskal on post_invocation_share: p=5.47e-89, epsilon^2=0.069; vs Community: p_adj=0.0194, rbc=0.205, vs GMD: p_adj=1.73e-09, rbc=0.665, vs Third-Party: p_adj=3.91e-11, rbc=0.748. stored='Custom', winner='Custom'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored by strong post-invocation share with non-dominant execution share.

observations.rq_title: Deployment context and run-level verdict usability
observations.obs_title: Styles differ in usable run-level verdict rates, although the overall separation is modest
observations.question: Which style currently has the strongest usable run-level verdict rate?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Chi-square for usable_verdict_rate: p=4.89e-17, Cramer's V=0.095; stored='GMD', winner='GMD'.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored by highest usable-verdict rate on first-attempt runs.

observations.rq_title: Deployment context and run-level verdict usability
observations.obs_title: Among usable verdicts, styles differ sharply in success rate
observations.question: Which style currently has the strongest success rate among usable verdicts?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Chi-square for success_among_usable: p=6.2e-252, Cramer's V=0.386; stored='GMD', winner='GMD'.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored by highest success rate among usable first-attempt runs.

observations.rq_title: Deployment context and run-level verdict usability
observations.obs_title: Styles are deployed in markedly different CI trigger contexts
observations.question: Do the styles remain deployed in markedly different CI trigger contexts?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Chi-square on style × event: p=0, Cramer's V=0.395; stored answer='Yes'.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Validated via style × trigger/event distribution difference.

observations.rq_title: Deployment context and run-level verdict usability
observations.obs_title: For Third-Party and Custom, success rate is strongly trigger-conditioned
observations.question: Which styles remain strongly trigger-conditioned in success behavior?
observations.L1_note_2026_03_27: Trigger-conditioned spread gap=0.3188; stored='Third-Party', winner='Third-Party'. Scored by the largest trigger-conditioned success-rate spread.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_03_27: Scored by the largest trigger-conditioned success-rate spread.
